events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes undersized motor overloads staged for a motor circuit installation. NEC Art. 430 governs motor circuits, including overload protection (Part III, Art. 430.32). Undersized overloads would trip prematurely or fail to protect properly. Actually, "undersized" overloads - if the overload rating is too small for the motor's full-load current, it will nuisance trip. If too large, it won't protect. Either way, non-compliant. Since work is staged but materials are wrong, this is a fail - the materials staged are defective/non-compliant.

The corrective action: select overload elements sized per NEC 430.32 based on motor nameplate full-load current (typically 115-125% of FLC for motors with service factor ≥1.15 or temp rise ≤40°C, up to 140% if necessary to start).

Component condition: non-compliant (staged materials are wrong). Could also be "installed-defective" but since staged, not installed yet. The categories given include "installed-defective" and "non-compliant". Since staged, "non-compliant" fits best.
